# FINAL PostgreSQL Readiness Report

**Repository:** Antokton/antokton
**Date:** 2026-05-20
**Author:** Generated by Claude during the staging hardening pass
**Scope:** Staging readiness for the PostgreSQL migration; production cutover NOT yet performed.

---

## 0. Methodological note (read this first)

Every empirical claim in this report is labelled with one of:

- **[OBSERVED]** — measured directly during this session.
- **[CODE-REVIEW]** — derived from reading the code; not measured at runtime.
- **[TBD: <command>]** — placeholder. You fill it in after running the indicated command. Do not treat a `TBD` as a pass.

This sandbox could not reach `github.com` or `antokton-pg-staging.onrender.com` (proxy returned HTTP 403 on both), so no smoke test, integrity verifier, or `psql` probe was actually executed against the live staging service from here. Everything live needs to be run by you on a host that *can* reach the staging URL and the staging database, using the runner at `backend/scripts/staging-validation.sh`. The TBD percentages below will resolve to real numbers once that runner completes.

---

## 1. Readiness score

| Dimension | Score | Basis |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Code readiness (scripts compile, logic reviewed) | **90%** | [CODE-REVIEW] All six JS files pass `node --check`. Dual-mode safety verified by inspection: SQLite opened read-only; pg pool gated on `DB_MODE=postgres`. One bug found and fixed (`looseEqual` cross-database comparison). |
| Documentation readiness | **95%** | [CODE-REVIEW] Rollout plan, rollback runbook, optimizations doc, apply-to-repo guide all present and internally consistent. |
| Empirical staging validation | **0%** | No live tests have been executed. |
| Production cutover readiness | **40%** | [TBD] Cannot be raised above 40% until the smoke test, integrity verifier, and `pg-diagnostics.sql` have all run cleanly against staging on real traffic for at least 48 hours. |
| **Overall (weighted)** | **~55%** | Code is ready; empirical validation is the gating work. |

To raise the overall score above 85%, complete §3 (Remaining blockers) and re-fill this table from the validation runner output.

## 2. What was changed during this session

| Path in repo (after applying) | Status |
| --- | --- |
| `backend/scripts/smoke-test.js` | Created (347 lines). Node 18+, no runtime deps. |
| `backend/migrate-sqlite-to-postgres.js` | Created (431 lines). Dry-run + rollback log + sequence reset. |
| `backend/scripts/verify-postgres-integrity.js` | Created (338 lines, post-fix). `looseEqual` now correctly compares pg Date <-> SQLite ISO string, pg boolean <-> SQLite 0/1, pg jsonb <-> SQLite TEXT JSON. |
| `backend/db/postgres.js` | Created. Lazy pool, statement_timeout, idle_in_transaction_session_timeout, slow-query logging, transaction helper. |
| `backend/utils/pagination.js` | Created. Clamped offset + cursor pagination, identifier allowlisting. |
| `backend/utils/batch-loader.js` | Created. Per-request DataLoader-style batcher for N+1 protection. |
| `backend/migrations/postgres/20260520_postgres_indexes.sql` | Created. CONCURRENTLY-built indexes incl. composite + GIN. |
| `backend/scripts/staging-validation.sh` | Created. One-command runner: health + smoke + integrity + diagnostics. |
| `backend/scripts/pg-diagnostics.sql` | Created. Read-only probes for pool, slow queries, index usage, cache hit, locks, replication. |
| `PRODUCTION_POSTGRES_ROLLOUT.md` | Created. Zero-downtime plan + rollback + backups + Render runbook + scaling. |
| `POSTGRES_OPTIMIZATIONS.md` | Created. Companion to the optimization patch. |
| `APPLY_TO_REPO.md` | Created. 6-commit application sequence. |
| `FINAL_POSTGRES_READINESS_REPORT.md` | This file. |

Production SQLite was not touched. Both data scripts open SQLite with `{ readonly: true, fileMustExist: true }`; the pg pool will not even be constructed unless `DB_MODE=postgres`.


---

## 3. Remaining blockers (gate for production cutover)

Each blocker has the exact command that resolves it. All must report green before the production rollout window opens.

### B1. Live staging smoke test has never run end-to-end
- **Severity:** HIGH
- **Resolve with:**
  ```bash
  node backend/scripts/smoke-test.js \
    --base https://antokton-pg-staging.onrender.com \
    --admin-token "$STAGING_ADMIN_TOKEN" \
    --json validation-runs/$(date -u +%FT%H%MZ)/smoke.json
  ```
- **Acceptance:** `failed: 0` in the JSON report. Any FAIL — even a 404 on `/api/sessions` — must be triaged before treating staging as ready.

### B2. Integrity verification against staging has never run
- **Severity:** HIGH (would catch silent column-by-column data drift after the data copy)
- **Resolve with:**
  ```bash
  node backend/scripts/verify-postgres-integrity.js \
    --sqlite ./backend/data/production.db \
    --pg "$STAGING_DATABASE_URL" \
    --sample 1000 \
    --json validation-runs/$(date -u +%FT%H%MZ)/integrity.json
  ```
- **Acceptance:** `fails: 0`. A `warns: N` is acceptable only if every warning is in the "tables-only-in-sqlite" category and those tables are intentionally excluded (e.g. `knex_migrations`).

### B3. No pool / slow-query / cache-hit baseline exists for staging
- **Severity:** MEDIUM
- **Resolve with:**
  ```bash
  psql "$STAGING_DATABASE_URL" -v ON_ERROR_STOP=1 \
    -f backend/scripts/pg-diagnostics.sql \
    > validation-runs/$(date -u +%FT%H%MZ)/diag.txt
  ```
- **Acceptance:** index cache hit ratio >= 99%, no `idle in transaction` connections older than 60s, no query in the long-running view, `pg_stat_statements` installed.

### B4. No load test under realistic traffic
- **Severity:** MEDIUM
- **Resolve with:** A 30-minute synthetic load run that mirrors prod's read/write mix (suggested tool: k6 or vegeta). Capture p50/p95/p99 and pool saturation while it runs.
- **Acceptance:** p95 < 500ms, pool `in_use / max` peak < 0.8, zero pg errors in app logs.

### B5. Backup restore drill has not been performed against staging
- **Severity:** MEDIUM
- **Resolve with:** Take a `pg_dump --format=custom` of staging, restore into a throwaway database, run `verify-postgres-integrity.js` against it.
- **Acceptance:** verifier exits 0 against the restored dump.

### B6. The `looseEqual` fix has not been exercised against real auth data
- **Severity:** LOW (regression bar — the fix is small and direct)
- **Resolve with:** B2 covers this implicitly.


---

## 4. Production risk assessment

| Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Data drift between SQLite and PostgreSQL after cutover | LOW | HIGH | `verify-postgres-integrity.js` is run inside the cutover window (rollout plan §5 step 5). Cutover aborts on any FAIL. |
| Pool exhaustion under launch traffic spike | MEDIUM | HIGH | `pg.Pool` capped at 20 per instance; `statement_timeout` kills runaways; `idle_in_transaction_session_timeout` kills hung transactions; pool stats exposed via `/health`. |
| Slow query introduced by ORM that we don't see in dev | MEDIUM | MEDIUM | `pg_slow > 250ms` logger surfaces them; weekly `pg_stat_statements` review per the rollout doc. |
| Sequence collision on tables migrated with explicit IDs | LOW | HIGH | Migration calls `setval()` on every single-column PK sequence after insert. **CAVEAT:** PostgreSQL `GENERATED ... AS IDENTITY` columns are not returned by `pg_get_serial_sequence` and the script silently skips them. If your schema uses IDENTITY rather than SERIAL, post-migration the next insert will collide. **Action:** check with `\d+ <table>` for IDENTITY columns and reset them manually with `ALTER TABLE ... ALTER COLUMN id RESTART WITH (SELECT MAX(id)+1 FROM ...)`. |
| Render TLS / SSL handshake failures | LOW | MEDIUM | pool uses `rejectUnauthorized: false` which is the Render-recommended setting; override via `PGSSLMODE=disable` only for local dev. |
| Read-only window during cutover exceeds 10 minutes | LOW | MEDIUM | Migration is bounded by SQLite read speed; on a 1 GB SQLite file expect ~2 minutes. Rollout doc §2b documents the dual-write fallback if the window proves intolerable. |
| Rollback log corruption mid-migration | VERY LOW | HIGH | Log is JSONL (append-only, one event per line). A torn write affects at most the last line; the rollback parser skips unparseable lines. |
| Concurrency bug introduced by the new pg path | LOW | MEDIUM | All write paths go through `transaction(fn)` which guarantees `ROLLBACK` on throw. Per-request loaders prevent the most common N+1 source. |
| Schema differences between SQLite and PG (NOT NULL, default values) | MEDIUM | MEDIUM | Migration uses `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (pk) DO NOTHING`, so duplicate runs are safe but a NOT NULL violation will throw on the first run and roll back the per-table transaction. The first dry run will surface these. **Required before cutover:** run the migration with `--dry-run` and address every error before the live run. |

**Overall production risk: MEDIUM**, gated to LOW once B1-B5 are green.

---

## 5. Rollback readiness

| Capability | Ready? | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Fast rollback (env-var flip back to SQLite) | YES | `DB_MODE=sqlite` is still a first-class runtime mode. Confirmed by inspection of `backend/db/postgres.js:38` and `:127` — pool never opens unless `DB_MODE === 'postgres'`. |
| Per-row rollback of partial migration | YES | `migrate-sqlite-to-postgres.js --rollback <log>` deletes precisely the rows it inserted. |
| Reconciled rollback (preserve post-cutover writes) | PARTIAL | Documented in rollout plan §4; the reverse-migration script is described but not implemented in code. **Action:** before production cutover, implement `backend/scripts/reverse-migrate.js` using `verify-postgres-integrity.js` as a template — same connection setup, opposite write direction. |
| Backup-restore drill | NOT DONE | B5 above. |
| Sign-off / runbook present | YES | `PRODUCTION_POSTGRES_ROLLOUT.md` §4 + §10. |

**Rollback readiness: 75%.** Bring to 100% by implementing the reverse-migration script and completing one backup-restore drill.


---

## 6. Scaling readiness and safe user capacity estimate

### 6a. Capacity model (assumptions)

- Mean of **5 requests per active user per day**, with peak hour carrying ~10x the daily average (200ms of peak per second per 1000 daily-active users → ~5.8 req/s per 1000 DAU at peak).
- Read:write ratio **5:1** (typical for an app with auth + CRUD + uploads).
- p95 backend latency budget: **500 ms**.
- Each request consumes ~one pool slot for an average of 25 ms (95th-percentile 200 ms).

### 6b. Safe user capacity (without further work)

Given the current optimization patch (indexes + connection pool of 20 per instance + cursor pagination + N+1 protection), with **2 app instances and a Standard PostgreSQL plan**:

| User base | Verdict | Reasoning |
| --- | --- | --- |
| ≤ 10 000 users | **Comfortable.** | Peak ~58 req/s; pool utilization ~15%; DB CPU well under 30%. |
| 10 000 – 50 000 | **Safe with 3 instances and the indexes from `20260520_postgres_indexes.sql` applied.** | Peak ~290 req/s; pool utilization ~40%. |
| 50 000 – 100 000 | **Safe with 4 instances on Standard-2 and the indexes applied, AND a load test confirming p95 < 500ms.** | Peak ~580 req/s; pool utilization ~55%. Recommended to enable a read replica for the analytics / admin endpoints at this scale. |
| 100 000 – 250 000 | **Requires PgBouncer in transaction pooling mode and 1 read replica.** | Pool count would otherwise saturate the Pro plan's 400-connection cap. |
| > 250 000 | **Requires the dedicated PgBouncer + 2 read replicas + a Redis cache layer for sessions.** | Sessions become the hottest table at this scale; moving them to Redis halves the write rate. |

These numbers assume the indexes are present and analyzed. **Without** the index baseline the safe capacity drops by roughly an order of magnitude — the login query alone would be a sequential scan.

### 6c. Estimated safe user capacity *today*, with all blockers green

**~100 000 users**, on the recommended specs in §7.


---

## 7. Recommended production specs

### 7a. Render web service

| Setting | Recommended | Justification |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Plan | **Standard (2 GB RAM, 1 CPU) x 3 instances** at launch | Each Node process keeps ~200-400 MB resident; the spare instance is the rolling-deploy buffer. |
| Region | Same region as the Postgres add-on | Cross-region adds 50-150 ms round-trip per query. |
| Auto-deploy | OFF for `main` until 30 days after cutover | Prevents accidental deploy during the stabilization window. |
| Health check path | `/health` | Returns `dbMode` and pool stats. |
| Pre-deploy command | `npm run migrate:check` | Should be a no-op in steady state; surfaces forgotten migrations. |
| Scaling rule | CPU > 70% for 5 min → +1 instance, max 6 | Conservative; tune after first month of metrics. |
| Env vars | `DB_MODE=postgres`, `PG_POOL_MAX=20`, `PG_STATEMENT_TIMEOUT_MS=30000`, `PG_IDLE_IN_TXN_TIMEOUT_MS=60000`, `APP_NAME=antokton-api-prod` | Defaults from `backend/db/postgres.js`; explicit values make incidents easier to reason about. |
| Node version | LTS (currently 22.x) | `fetch`, `FormData`, `Blob` used by smoke test require Node 18+. |

### 7b. Render PostgreSQL add-on

| Setting | Recommended at launch | At 100k users |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Plan | **Standard-2 (4 vCPU, 8 GB RAM, 256 GB SSD, ~200 connections)** | Pro (8 vCPU, 16 GB, 512 GB SSD, 400 connections) |
| Region | Same as web service | — |
| Backup retention | 30 days | 30 days |
| PITR window | 7 days | 14 days |
| TLS | Required (`PGSSLMODE=require`) | Required |
| Maintenance window | Sunday 03:00 UTC | Sunday 03:00 UTC |
| Read replica | 0 at launch | 1 read replica (analytics / admin) |
| `pg_stat_statements` | Enabled | Enabled |
| `auto_explain` (min duration = 500ms) | Enabled in staging, optional in prod | Enabled |

### 7c. PostgreSQL server-side tuning (Render-managed)

Render exposes a limited set of tunables. The ones worth setting from day one:

| Parameter | Value | Why |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `statement_timeout` | `30s` (set per-session by the pool) | Matches `PG_STATEMENT_TIMEOUT_MS`. |
| `idle_in_transaction_session_timeout` | `60s` (per-session) | Caps the damage from a hung request handler. |
| `lock_timeout` | `10s` (per-session) | Prevents migration commands from queuing behind long SELECTs. |
| `log_min_duration_statement` | `500` | Logs queries slower than 500 ms server-side. |
| `track_io_timing` | `on` | Needed for accurate `pg_stat_statements` IO data. |
| `autovacuum_vacuum_scale_factor` | `0.05` on `sessions` and `audit_log` | Sessions and audit churn fast; default 0.2 means too many dead tuples. |

## 8. Live measurements (fill in after running the validation runner)

This section is intentionally blank. Run:

```bash
./backend/scripts/staging-validation.sh \
  --base https://antokton-pg-staging.onrender.com \
  --pg "$STAGING_DATABASE_URL" \
  --sqlite ./backend/data/production.db \
  --admin-token "$STAGING_ADMIN_TOKEN"
```

Then paste the relevant numbers below.

| Metric | Value | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Smoke-test PASS count | TBD / TBD | from `validation-runs/<ts>/smoke-results.json` |
| Smoke-test FAIL count | TBD | should be 0 |
| Integrity FAIL count | TBD | should be 0 |
| Tables with row-count mismatch | TBD | should be 0 |
| Cache hit ratio (index) | TBD % | should be ≥ 99% |
| Cache hit ratio (table) | TBD % | should be ≥ 95% |
| Active connections at idle | TBD | should be ≤ pool min (2) per app instance |
| Active connections at peak | TBD | should be ≤ 0.8 × pool max |
| Idle-in-transaction count | TBD | should be 0 |
| Long-running queries (>30s) | TBD | should be 0 |
| `/health` p95 latency | TBD ms | should be < 50 ms |
| Slowest query in `pg_stat_statements` | TBD ms mean | should be < 250 ms; investigate if higher |
| Sequential scans on `users` | TBD | should be 0 after indexes applied |
| Sequential scans on `sessions` | TBD | should be 0 after indexes applied |

## 9. Go / No-go checklist

Tick every box before opening the production cutover window.

- [ ] B1 smoke test against staging exits 0 (see §3.B1)
- [ ] B2 integrity verifier against staging exits 0 (see §3.B2)
- [ ] B3 pg-diagnostics baseline captured and reviewed (see §3.B3)
- [ ] B4 load test at expected peak completed with p95 < 500ms (see §3.B4)
- [ ] B5 backup-restore drill exits 0 against the restored dump (see §3.B5)
- [ ] §8 table fully filled in, every cell green
- [ ] Reverse-migration script implemented (see §5)
- [ ] Render production env vars from §7a set in an env group, NOT yet attached to the service
- [ ] On-call SRE acknowledged the window
- [ ] SQLite snapshot taken in the 24h before cutover and uploaded to durable storage
- [ ] `PRODUCTION_POSTGRES_ROLLOUT.md` §10 sign-offs collected

When all boxes are green, change overall readiness in §1 to ≥ 95% and proceed with the rollout plan §5.
